title: Rhaphis acicularis
taxonomy: Rhaphis acicularis is classified as a type of flowering plant, belonging to the family Bromeliaceae. It is a species of bromeliad, a group of plants known for their unique, often epiphytic growth habits. More specifically, Rhaphis acicularis is classified as a genus within the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics, as well as its genetic relationships with other species. The exact classification of Rhaphis acicularis is subject to ongoing research and refinement.

intro: Rhaphis acicularis is a plant species, also known as "Rhaphis grass," that has been traditionally used in various folk medicines. It has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rhaphis acicularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
